name: lrmem8e4_seqlen128_exp022
hypothesis: every higher-LR run (4e-4+) diverged at seq_len=512 due to state accumulation over 512 tokens; at seq_len=128 the state accumulates 4x less, so the top-of-range lr_memory 8e-4 (never tried) might stay stable AND push hard enough on optimization to break the format plateau. Tests the LR x length interaction never directly probed: if tokacc climbs at 128+8e-4, length-mediated the LR divergence and high LR can retrieve; if it diverges even at 128, divergence is LR-driven not length-driven (confirms exp006); if stable-flat, plateau is fundamental regardless of optimization strength.
